summary: DHH updates his criticism of Google Web Accelerator (GWA) after learning that the missing X-moz:prefetch header was a bug, not a deliberate removal. Google's product manager Othman Laraki contacted DHH directly to explain the issue and confirm a fix was being rolled out. While DHH acknowledges this is good news, he maintains his broader criticism of the GWA concept itself. He uses the new contact to push for more answers about Google's motives.
key_points: The missing prefetch header in GWA requests was confirmed as a bug by Google's product manager, not intentional removal
Google began rolling out a fix immediately after DHH's post brought attention to the issue
DHH maintains that GWA is fundamentally problematic even with the bug fixed
Open dialogue between developers and product teams can lead to quick resolutions
money_quotes: So while I still consider the GWA to be pretty evil stuff, it's at least not double evil by intention.
Pretty nasty bug, but shit happens every where.
Now this naturally doesn't exonerate the vanilla evilness of the GWA concept, but its definitely a step in the right direction.
key_insight: A missing HTTP header that broke web application safeguards against Google Web Accelerator turned out to be a bug rather than malice, illustrating that assuming incompetence over evil intent is usually the right call.
